items.briefing:
Microsoft has released its largest-ever monthly security update, patching a record 570 vulnerabilities across its products. Australian organisations using Microsoft software — which is most of us — should ensure updates are applied as soon as possible, as the sheer volume makes it harder for IT teams to prioritise the most critical fixes.

Separately, security researchers at Check Point warn that cybercriminals are now using AI to actively assist in live attacks, making threats faster and harder to detect. Be extra cautious with unexpected emails or login requests right now.

**Practical tip:** Check with your IT support that Microsoft's July updates have been applied to your devices this week.

featured.title: AI now runs parts of cyber attacks, Check Point says
featured.quote: AI-accelerated cyber attacks demand faster Australian security responses and patching strategies.
